description: The figure presents the polarization resistance ($R_\mathrm{p}$) values derived from electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) fittings as a function of immersion time (0–168 h) for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs) subjected to different solution treatments. Four samples are compared: S0 (as-hot-rolled without solution treatment), S950 (solution-treated at 950°C), S1050 (1050°C), and S1150 (1150°C), represented by red stars, green circles, blue triangles, and pink squares respectively. The fine-grained S950 sample exhibits the highest $R_\mathrm{p}$ values, rapidly increasing to approximately $1.2\times10^5$ Ω·cm² within 24 h and stabilizing thereafter at 168 h (114.9 kΩ·cm²), indicating rapid formation of a dense, protective Al-rich oxide film. In contrast, the coarse-grained S1150 and S0 samples show significantly lower $R_\mathrm{p}$ values ($\sim$4–$5\times10^4$ Ω·cm²) with slower growth rates, suggesting delayed and less protective film formation. The S1050 sample displays intermediate performance ($\sim$8×10⁴ Ω·cm²). This demonstrates that grain refinement (8.7 μm for S950 vs. 32 μm for S1150) enhances corrosion resistance by promoting uniform oxide film formation through increased grain boundary density, which facilitates preferential Al dissolution and rapid development of a continuous protective barrier.
